themes.title: AI agents and coding
themes.summary: Copilot, skills, context, feedback, and practical work with agents in software development.

themes.title: AI product and business
themes.summary: How user interfaces, pricing, consumption, and business models change in AI-first products.

themes.title: Personal AI workflow
themes.summary: Practical personal workflows for using AI to accelerate learning, reading, and work with information.

articles.title: Saving tokens in GitHub Copilot
articles.eyebrow: How to reduce costs in 15 minutes
articles.subtitle: From easy wins to advanced context engineering. The goal is not to spend the fewest tokens, but to get maximum value from each one.
articles.summary: Practical techniques for reducing token consumption in agentic coding without losing result quality.

articles.title: How to centrally manage and govern skills for agents
articles.subtitle: Download a central skill, create a local PoC when it needs improvement, send the proposal to GitHub, let agents triage and prepare a PR after human approval, and distribute the improved skill to everyone.
articles.summary: A workflow proposal for team-managed agent skills through a catalog, local PoC, GitHub issue, and agentic triage.

articles.title: Is subscription a suitable business model for an AI product? Is AI getting more expensive? Are tokens getting more expensive? Or are we just moving toward a fair model?
articles.subtitle: Flat subscription is great for adoption, but agents break the natural limits of consumption. I see the future more in a license as an entry ticket, pooled prepaid consumption, and fair top-up units.
articles.summary: A reflection on why agents change the economics of AI products and why a pure flat subscription model runs into limits.

articles.title: AI coding - context vs. measurement, software as memory, I will not learn your software, OpenClaw and the smart home
articles.eyebrow: A home experiment with AI agents
articles.subtitle: A home project with Home Assistant, Copilot CLI, and OpenClaw as a practical lab for thinking about context, feedback, software as memory, and GenUI.
articles.summary: A home experiment showing why context, measurement, feedback, and agentic UX matter for AI coding.

articles.title: Spiral reading: AI podcast from PDFs without losing context
articles.subtitle: How to build a simple workflow in which AI turns a PDF into an audiobook without losing the document's original structure.
articles.summary: A personal workflow for layered reading, listening, and asking AI questions about books, PDFs, and longer texts.

articles.title: Three paths to GenUI and their pros and cons
articles.subtitle: A static artifact, a declarative specification, or fully generated interactive code? Three practical paths to visual GenUI and their trade-offs.
articles.summary: A comparison of three paths to generated UI: static artifacts, declarative specifications, and fully generated interactive code.
